channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: France and Germany Halt €100B FCAS Fighter Jet Program
item.description: Long-running disputes over technology sharing, industrial workshare and intellectual property rights have forced France and Germany to scrap their landmark next-generation fighter jet project, dealing a major blow to European defense ambitions.

item.title: Israel Orders Evacuation of Lebanons Tyre Including Christian Quarter
item.description: The Israeli military issued an evacuation order for the Lebanese city of Tyre, including its Christian quarter, as hostilities with Hezbollah continue amid broader regional tensions.

item.title: Taiwan Reports China Coast Guard Harassing Commercial Shipping
item.description: Taiwans coast guard accused Chinas coast guard of harassing commercial shipping off its coast, telling merchant vessels to ignore Chinese inquiries in a new escalation of maritime tensions.

item.title: Chinas Exports Surge 19.4% Year-on-Year Despite War Conditions
item.description: Chinas exports posted a 19.4% year-on-year increase in May, defying expectations amid severe effects from ongoing conflict in the Middle East and global supply chain disruptions.

item.title: Russian Drone and Missile Strikes Kill 3 Injure 18 in Kharkiv Oblast
item.description: Russian forces launched 11 drone strikes on Kharkiv, with additional missile attacks killing 3 and injuring 18 as Ukraine continues to face sustained aerial bombardment.

item.title: Pentagon adds Alibaba, BYD, Baidu to Chinese military companies list
item.description: The US Defense Department added major Chinese tech firms including Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD to a list of companies believed to aid China's military, escalating tensions with Beijing and warning American businesses about potential risks.

item.title: US judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee as unlawful
item.description: A federal judge ruled that the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas for skilled foreign workers was an unlawful tax not authorized by Congress, blocking a policy that tech companies had opposed.

item.title: North Korea and China Agree to Expand Cooperation Across Multiple Sectors
item.description: North Korea and China signed agreements to expand cooperation across various sectors during high-level talks, deepening ties between the two countries as Pyongyang seeks to strengthen alliances amid ongoing regional tensions.

item.title: Powerful Earthquake Strikes Southern Philippines, Rescue Efforts Underway
item.description: A powerful earthquake struck the southern Philippines, trapping residents and prompting rescue efforts as authorities assess damage and casualties.

item.title: Somali Soccer Referee Denied US Entry Will Miss World Cup Debut
item.description: Somali referee Omar Artan was denied entry to the United States and will miss his World Cup debut, highlighting visa challenges facing international officials.

item.title: US Helicopter Crew Rescued After Going Down Near Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A US military helicopter went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz amid heightened tensions with Iran, with all crew members safely rescued.

item.title: Ebola Death Toll in DR Congo Climbs to 101
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has claimed 101 lives out of 550 cases reported since the outbreak was declared a month ago.

item.title: Investors See Tipping Point for Bonds After Geopolitics Shreds Old Playbooks
item.description: Global investors are reassessing bond strategies as geopolitical turmoil upends traditional market correlations and expectations.

item.title: UN Urges Afghan Taliban to End Arrests of Women Over Dress Rules
item.description: The United Nations called on Afghanistan's Taliban rulers to stop arresting women for alleged dress code violations, condemning restrictive policies.

item.title: WHO Chief Urges Uganda to Reconsider Congo Border Closure Over Ebola
item.description: WHO Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus said Uganda should reconsider its decision to close its border with Congo amid the Ebola outbreak.

item.title: France and Germany Cancel FCAS Fighter Jet Program
item.description: France and Germany have officially cancelled the Future Combat Air System (FCAS) program, a €100 billion joint fighter jet project, after failing to resolve disputes over technology sharing and industrial workshare.

item.title: UN Warns of Deepening Crisis in Oceans, Urges Action
item.description: The United Nations issued a stark warning about the deteriorating state of the world's oceans, calling for urgent action to address pollution, overfishing, and climate impacts.

item.title: Myanmar Is ASEAN Family Indonesia Tells Junta Chief Turned President
item.description: Indonesia reaffirmed that Myanmar remains part of the ASEAN family during talks with the junta chief-turned-president, pressing for progress on the regional peace plan.

item.title: UAE Is Changing Rhetoric Not Policy Toward Iran
item.description: Despite public statements suggesting a shift, the UAE's fundamental policy toward Iran remains unchanged as Gulf states navigate regional tensions.

item.title: Israel Seeks Sway Over Peace Talks After Brief Iran Fight Defying Trump
item.description: Israel launched brief strikes on Iran despite Trump's push for restraint, seeking to maintain influence over peace negotiations and regional dynamics.

item.title: ICC Prosecutor Suspended Pending Wider Vote on Misconduct Allegations
item.description: The International Criminal Court prosecutor has been suspended pending a broader vote on misconduct allegations, marking a significant crisis for the institution.

item.title: Russia Admits Fuel Supply Problems After Ukraine Targets Energy Sites
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov acknowledged fuel supply problems after Ukrainian strikes targeted Russian energy infrastructure.

item.title: IAEA Calls on Iran to Re-Engage With West Under Pressure Resolution
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency passed a resolution calling on Iran to re-engage with Western powers over its nuclear program.

item.title: Oil Rises Slightly as Investors Await Clarity After Iran-Israel Halt Attacks
item.description: Oil prices edged up as markets waited for clarity on the Middle East situation after Iran and Israel agreed to halt attacks.

item.title: Netanyahu and Trump at Odds Over Their War Whos Calling the Shots
item.description: Despite starting the Iran war in lockstep with Israel, the US president is pushing for a swift exit as pressure builds at home and abroad.
